title: daucus communis subsp. carota
intro: Daucus communis subsp. carota, also known as wild carrot or Queen Anne's lace, is a biennial plant belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae) and is closely related to the edible carrot (Daucus carota subsp. sativus). The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Daucus communis subsp. carota include relief from digestive issues, respiratory problems, and menstrual cramps, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels and reduce blood pressure. Wild carrot can be consumed as a fresh herb, used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, or dried and used in herbal preparations such as capsules and powders.
